creator: asomers@FreeBSD.org
text: Currently ctl.conf is required to be a single monolithic file.  It would be better if the file format supported an "include" directive, which would include the contents of another file at the current location.  Alternatively, it could include the contents of all files in a certain directory (just like syslog.conf's include directive).  That would be useful for servers that use iSCSI for multiple applications.
